curation_notes: Patch commit bb6fb30 modifies only common/hash.go, changing two functions (SHA512_256 and SHA512_256i) to add 8-byte little-endian length encoding after each element delimiter. The pre-assigned class crypto-weakness (CWE-327) is correct: the vulnerability is a flawed hash construction that uses a single-byte delimiter without per-element length encoding, allowing hash collisions between different input splits. No sibling files share the same vulnerability pattern; hash_utils.go contains only a RejectionSample helper, and SHA512_256iOne takes a single input so is not affected.
